# VeloWrite

VeloWrite is a private online Markdown editor and lightweight Tauri desktop app.

## Primary URLs

- Website: https://velowrite.app/
- Online editor: https://velowrite.app/web
- Desktop downloads: https://velowrite.app/download
- Interactive demo: https://velowrite.app/demo
- Pro roadmap: https://velowrite.app/pro
- Public feedback roadmap: https://velowrite.app/roadmap
- Markdown library: https://velowrite.app/docs
- Online Markdown Editor article: https://velowrite.app/docs/online-markdown-editor
- Markdown Basics article: https://velowrite.app/docs/markdown-basics
- Markdown guide: https://velowrite.app/guide
- Changelog: https://velowrite.app/changelog includes the version directory for 0.1.0 through 0.1.7.
- FAQ: https://velowrite.app/faq
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/ken-water/velowrite
- Markdown guide PDF: https://velowrite.app/markdown-guide.pdf

## Product Summary

VeloWrite helps writers, developers, students, and teams draft Markdown quickly in the browser, preview rendered output, export Markdown or HTML, and move serious local files to a lightweight desktop app built with Tauri. The site also includes a practical Markdown guide, a staged Markdown article library, changelog, and public feedback roadmap for preview updates.

The current public version is a free preview. It includes online editing, live preview, Markdown file download, HTML export, desktop open/save, recent files, local history snapshots, math rendering, code highlighting, and tabbed multi-language code previews.

## Positioning

- Category: Markdown editor, online Markdown editor, local-first writing app, lightweight desktop writing tool.
- Audience: developers, technical writers, product teams, students, documentation teams, and Markdown users who want a fast Typora-like workflow.
- Differentiation: fast web trial, local-first desktop workflow, privacy-focused editing, lightweight Tauri packaging, and planned AI, sync, and publishing workflows.

## Current Preview Limits

- Windows installers are unsigned.
- The macOS DMG is built separately and appears on the download page only after the GitHub release asset exists.
- AI assistant, private sync, account system, encrypted sharing, and one-click publishing are planned but not active in the current public build.
- Important files should be backed up while testing preview builds.

## FAQ

- What is VeloWrite? A private online Markdown editor and lightweight Tauri desktop app for fast writing, live preview, export, local history, and native file workflows.
- What can I read first? The Markdown library at https://velowrite.app/docs lists the currently published articles and the planned article queue. Start with Online Markdown Editor for the browser workflow, then read Markdown Basics for headings, lists, tables, code, and math examples. The changelog explains what changed across preview versions 0.1.0 through 0.1.7.
- Can I try VeloWrite without installing anything? Yes. Open the web editor in your browser and start writing immediately.
- What happens if I refresh the browser while editing? Your draft stays in localStorage in the same browser, so a refresh on the same device can bring it back.
- Who is VeloWrite for? Developers, technical writers, students, founders, and teams who write Markdown notes, documentation, specs, guides, blog drafts, or knowledge-base content.
- Is VeloWrite free to use today? The current public build is a free preview. Future AI, private sync, publishing automation, advanced exports, and team workflows may become Pro features.
- Does VeloWrite upload my Markdown documents? Normal web editing and preview do not upload Markdown document content to VeloWrite servers. Browser drafts stay in localStorage, and desktop files and history snapshots stay on your device by default.
- What is the difference between web and desktop? Web is best for quick drafts, preview, Markdown download, and HTML export. Desktop is better for real local files, native open and save, offline work, recent files, and local history snapshots.
- Does VeloWrite handle math, tables, and code highlighting? Yes. The preview supports Markdown tables, KaTeX math rendering, syntax-highlighted code blocks, and tabbed previews for multi-language code examples.
- Where can users see recorded feedback? The public roadmap at https://velowrite.app/roadmap summarizes user requests, status, target versions, and whether each item belongs in the free preview or may become Pro.
- What will VeloWrite Pro include? The planned Pro direction includes AI writing commands, private sync, one-click publishing, advanced exports, themes, and commercial workflows. Pricing will be published before checkout opens.

## Privacy Notes

Normal browser editing and preview do not upload Markdown document content to VeloWrite servers. Browser drafts, preferences, and analytics consent are stored locally in the user's browser. Desktop files and local history snapshots stay on the user's device by default.

Waitlist and feedback forms send submitted contact details and message fields to Loops.so for beta communication and product feedback management.

## Suggested Description

VeloWrite is a private online Markdown editor and lightweight Tauri desktop app for fast writing, live preview, export, local history, and native file workflows.
